description: Typography expresses hierarchy and brand presence. The scale uses a minimal set of styles to define visual hierarchy. Font family is Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif. Two font weights are available: Regular (400) and Bold (700).

always: Apply a type scale class (t-18, t-16, t-14, t-12) to establish size — the HTML tag alone does not set the correct size.
Use fw-bold or font-weight-bold (700) for bold emphasis. Do not invent other weight values.
Use semantic HTML elements (h1–h6, p, strong, em) and layer type scale classes on top for visual sizing.
Combine text color classes (text-primary, text-secondary, text-muted, etc.) with type scale classes to create hierarchy without changing size.
never: Do not use inline `style` attributes to set font sizes — use the t-{n} utility classes instead.
Do not use small-bolded-text on anything other than button labels or button-like UI — it is uppercase and bold by design.
Do not skip heading levels for layout reasons — use a type scale class on the correct semantic element instead.
Do not use font-weight values other than 400 (normal) or 700 (bold).

typeScale.description: Apply these classes to any semantic HTML element (h1–h6, p, span, div) to set font size, letter spacing, and line height. The HTML tag controls semantics; the class controls appearance.

t-18.usage: Large Header — use to overstate or create maximum emphasis at section level.

t-16.usage: Regular Header — default heading size. Use for card titles, panel headings, and subsection labels.

t-14.usage: Regular Paragraph / Small Header — default body text size. Also used for downplayed headers that should read as labels.

t-12.usage: Small Paragraph — use for captions, helper text, metadata, and secondary information that should recede.

small-bolded-text.usage: Button labels and button-like UI text only. Uppercase bold — do not use for general body copy.

textColors.description: Combine color classes with type scale classes to create hierarchy. Color classes do not change size.

text-dark.usage: High-contrast body text on light backgrounds.

text-muted.usage: Placeholder text, secondary metadata, disabled labels.

text-white.usage: Text on dark or colored backgrounds.

textTransform.text-capitalize: Capitalizes the first letter of each word.

textAlignment.note: Responsive variants are available: text-sm-start, text-md-center, text-lg-end, etc.

textUtilities.text-truncate: Truncates overflowing text with an ellipsis. Requires a fixed width on the element.
textUtilities.text-nowrap: Prevents text from wrapping to the next line.
textUtilities.text-break: Breaks long words or URLs that would otherwise overflow.
textUtilities.text-reset: Resets text color to inherit from parent.

semanticElements.description: Use semantic HTML for meaning, then apply type scale classes for visual sizing.
semanticElements.strong: Bold emphasis — renders at font-weight 700. Use for critical keywords.
semanticElements.em: Italic emphasis. Use sparingly for stress emphasis.
semanticElements.small: Renders slightly smaller than surrounding text. Prefer t-12 for precise control.
semanticElements.mark: Highlights text with a background color. Use for search result highlights.
semanticElements.blockquote: Block-level quotation. Use class `blockquote` for Bootstrap styling.
semanticElements.code: Inline code snippet. Renders in monospace font.

SubsectionLabel.description: Small label heading that sits above content groups.
